seoTitle: Mount Austin vs Mount Colah — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Austin and Mount Colah. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Austin vs Mount Colah: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Austin and Mount Colah.
heading: Mount Austin vs Mount Colah — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Austin, New South Wales and Mount Colah, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Austin has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount Colah (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Austin or Mount Colah safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Austin has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Mount Colah scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Austin scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Colah scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Austin or Mount Colah?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Austin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
